intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers calming, intimate atmospheres over clinical or stark digital interfaces
facade.label: Soft warm light
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm color palettes (amber, soft gold, warm grays) and gentle lighting effects to create a soothing, inviting mood
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cool, neutral, or bright clinical aesthetics that feel more objective and data-forward than emotionally atmospheric

facade.hypothesis: Users building personal health experiments respond better to friendly, supportive language that celebrates progress over clinical or neutral phrasing
facade.label: Warm & encouraging tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational, celebratory language throughout the dashboard—'You crushed it!' instead of 'Data logged'
facade.rejectImplies: Keep tone neutral and clinical—prioritize clarity and objectivity over emotional warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer prominent metrics (weight, blood pressure, step count) displayed large with minimal supporting context, letting them scan status at a glance
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — is the user a glancer or a grazer?
facade.label: Big numbers, small text
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should lead with bold, large KPIs; supporting detail available but de-emphasized
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should embed metrics within narrative context (trends, comparisons, notes) so no number stands alone

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers calming, intimate atmospheres over clinical or stark digital interfaces
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use warm color palettes (amber, soft gold, warm grays) and gentle lighting effects to create a soothing, inviting mood

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual motion between states over instant changes, especially for data that shifts incrementally over time
facade.axisTargeted: Animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth fade transitions
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use easing and duration to soften data updates, metric changes, and view switches—creating a sense of continuity
facade.rejectImplies: The dashboard should prioritize immediate feedback with zero-latency state changes, keeping focus on data accuracy over motion polish

facade.hypothesis: Users want a persistent left navigation that contains tabbed sections for different experiment categories, reducing overall navigation depth
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation pattern (sidebar vs. tabs vs. hybrid)
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: Explore hybrid structures where sidebar and tabs work together; test persistent nav + nested grouping
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward either pure sidebar or pure tab-based navigation; assume users prefer flat or sequential organization

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health data prefer high-contrast interfaces that reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins
facade.label: Dark cards, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use dark mode with bright typography for readability and reduced cognitive load
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should explore lighter backgrounds, possibly with softer contrast or colored surfaces instead

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, personal interaction methods (like sketching or writing notes) over purely digital input, which reinforces the warm, intimate mood preference and suggests they want their health experiments to feel like a personal journal rather than a clinical tracker
facade.label: Handwritten data entry
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should include sketch/handwriting UI elements, natural input methods, and a journal-like aesthetic that preserves the feeling of personal reflection over machine-like data collection
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, precise digital input—forms, dropdowns, buttons. This would suggest the warm atmosphere is about visual design, not interaction philosophy, and they want frictionless efficiency despite the intimate mood preference

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
